lesson.menu_title: Now and Always -- Present Simple vs. Present Continuous
lesson.menu_desc: Habit or now? Choose the right tense at the official dinner -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- Now and Always -- Present Simple vs. Present Continuous
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 5 -- Present Simple vs. Present Continuous

listenings.text: Hello, Maria Clara. This is Sofia, your colleague from the international office. I am calling with a quick update about this week. Usually, we meet on Mondays, but this week is different. Right now, the delegation is arriving from China, so we are changing the schedule. Normally you present in the morning, but today you are presenting after lunch. The provost is waiting for you at the hotel at seven. I am confirming your dinner appointment now. Have a good day, and see you soon.

Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the education conference. Let me give you a short update on today's program. Every day, the conference starts at nine o'clock. This morning, we are starting a little later, because many guests are still arriving. At the moment, our team is preparing the main room. Usually, the first speaker talks for thirty minutes, but today Maria Clara is presenting a special session about leadership. Please confirm your seat with a colleague at the front desk. Thank you, and enjoy the day.

extra_audio.text: Hello. My name is Maria Clara. Usually, I travel with a big delegation. But this week, I am traveling alone. Right now, I am preparing for an important dinner in Seoul.

items.situation: A colleague asks: "How is your normal week?"
tips: I usually travel with a delegation, and I meet my team every month.
A routine: present simple + usually / every

items.situation: The provost asks: "What are you doing this week?"
tips: This week, I am attending a conference in Seoul.
Happening now: present continuous + this week
Yes, I live and work in Maceio, in Brazil.

items.situation: At dinner, the waiter offers fish or meat.
tips: It depends on the day, but tonight I would rather have the fish.
Preference: It depends on... / I would rather...

items.situation: A guest asks: "What is your team doing right now?"
tips: At the moment, my team is preparing a big report.
Happening now: present continuous + at the moment

items.situation: The provost speaks fast and you miss a word.
tips: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Could you repeat, please?
Asking to repeat is control, not weakness.
